page: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor is equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography.

This server includes 614 GB of local SSD storage and is premium storage capable, making it suitable for workloads requiring fast local scratch space. In benchmark evaluations, the E16ds_v4 demonstrates consistent mid-tier performance. It achieved a single-core Geekbench score of 1244.0 and a multi-core score of 7658.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 830.49 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths reached 69.79 GB/sec and 41.6 GB/sec, respectively.

The system is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching layers, as evidenced by its Redis SET performance of 13.72 million operations per second and a Passmark database score of 5749.22. However, it exhibits clear limitations in compute-heavy AI tasks; while it handles small 135M LLM models adequately, prompt processing for a medium 7B model drops to a weak 29.49 tokens per second. The absence of a GPU and the 1:8 memory-to-core ratio position this instance as a cost-efficient choice for enterprise databases, web serving, and memory-bound data processing rather than heavy parallel computing.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It provides 16 dedicated vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server supports AVX-512 instructions. Benchmarks show average performance across the board, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7658.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 13.72 million operations per second. While highly capable for memory-intensive applications, its CPU-only architecture shows performance trade-offs in complex tasks, such as a weak prompt processing speed of 29.49 tokens per second on 7B LLM models. It is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v4 is a memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local SSD. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL CPU and is designed for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v4 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local SSD storage on Intel Xeon hardware.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, 128 GB RAM, and local SSD storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB RAM providing a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio
614 GB of bundled local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI instruction support
Strong database performance with 13.72 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
